Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible metabolic acidosis. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones) indicate renal involvement, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend urgent clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation.
